title: Improve error message when the entrypoint has the path "/run"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**



/kind feature

**Description**

If a container image has an executable installed at the filepath _/run_, the error message 

```
Error: OCI runtime error: open `/home/erikdev/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/08afa3c884c5580029b68b10edd4c6a706fdd49c45d4ead9469ad24308f64bde/merged/.containerenv`: Not a directory
```


could be made more informative when trying to run the container.



This feature request is not so important as it is just catching an edge case where a strangely built container is being used.
Feel free to close it. 


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Running rootless.

1. Run `podman build -t buggy . ` to build this Dockerflie
```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
RUN cp /bin/true /root/run

FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34

COPY --from=0 /root/run /

ENTRYPOINT ["/run"]
```

2.  Run `podman run --rm localhost/buggy`

```
$ podman run --rm localhost/buggy
Error: OCI runtime error: open `/home/erikdev/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/08afa3c884c5580029b68b10edd4c6a706fdd49c45d4ead9469ad24308f64bde/merged/.containerenv`: Not a directory
$ 
```



**Describe the results you received:**

```
`/home/erikdev/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/08afa3c884c5580029b68b10edd4c6a706fdd49c45d4ead9469ad24308f64bde/merged/.containerenv`: Not a directory
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

Just a sketch:
```
Error: Podman can not run the entrypoint /run. This path is expected to be a directory.
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68244469/podman-oci-containerenv-not-a-directory

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    - container_id: 0

      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1002
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.12.13-300.fc34.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 16346628096
  memTotal: 33503191040
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.20.1-1.fc34.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.20.1
      commit: 0d42f1109fd73548f44b01b3e84d04a279e99d2e

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1002/podman/podman.sock

    apparmorEnabled: false

    rootless: true
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.2-1.fc34.x86_64
```

comments.body: @eriksjolund Afaik following error is directly coming from the runtime and podman is just relaying. In order to print a custom error msg at podman level an extra `stat` call needs to happen at podman level for entrypoint before it goes to runtime.  But i  am not sure if extra `stat` is call worth the cost just for changing error message.

I don't see this as worth hacking around to fix it, since you could probably figure out other options in /run to break like creation of other inodes.

The .containerenv error is actually happening within podman when it attempts to create a file in the /run directory, but /run is not a directory.

I am going to close this as wontfix.
